description_long: This object is a detailed model of a slipper lobster, characterized by its broad, flattened body and a carapace that extends forward into a rounded, shield-like shape. The exoskeleton is a muted pinkish-orange with subtle mottling, and the surface is covered in small spines and ridges, especially along the edges of the carapace. The lobster has several pairs of segmented walking legs extending from beneath the body, and a short, fan-shaped tail at the rear. The head region is wide and flat, with small, closely set eyes and short, stubby antennae visible at the front. The underside reveals more delicate appendages and the segmentation between the cephalothorax and abdomen. The overall appearance is robust and armored, typical of marine crustaceans found in seafood markets or restaurants.
description: A broad, pinkish-orange slipper lobster with a flattened, spiny carapace and multiple segmented legs. Its shield-like head and short tail give it a distinctive, armored appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the slipper lobster, highlighting its segmented legs, broad carapace, and short tail.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the fan-shaped tail and the symmetrical segmentation of the abdomen, with legs visible underneath.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, emphasizing the curvature of the carapace and the arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the wide, shield-like head, small eyes, and short antennae, with the spiny edge of the carapace prominent.
